name: Blackbean Greenbeans
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp low sodium soy sauce or tamari
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the green beans and trim them into bite-sized pieces.
2. Steam or microwave the beans with a little water until just tender-crisp. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking.
3. Mix together the black bean paste, soy sauce, water, and corn starch in a bowl until very smooth.
4. Heat a wok or large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add some water and the cooked beans. Cook for about 2 minutes, then add the sauce.
5. Stir constantly to coat the beans with the sauce (it will become thick). Serve immediately over hot cooked rice.
This sauce and cooking process can be used with any vegetables you like. My husband enjoys this recipe when made with asparagus, which is typically $3.99 per pound.
